analysis: First score (7180.86 floor) - no baseline to compare; no pipeline edits. Lesson candidates parked for next improved round: (a) direct-API error-body read on CLI 400s already saved to memory; (b) grader-mirror-first workflow proved itself (0.10 delta).

v7 fill regime LB 10.08 to 36.63 (plus 26.55, 43pct of gap). Cause = deadline-aware per-model fill: fast gemma self-sizes N far beyond slow gpt_oss static cap, lifting mean(public rows) above any static-N (~32). This generalizable lesson is ALREADY in /kaggloop-submit code-comp rule 4; comp-specific knobs (SEARCH_FRACTION, prompt phrasing) stay in the journal. No new shared-pipeline edit warranted.
rationale: Results-ism gate passed (real plus 26.55) but the sole generalizable lesson (self-size per backend when the eval averages multi-speed backends) is already encoded, verified by re-reading rule 4. Incidental finding: selfimprove check mixes actual_source; a pre-LB CV upper-bound row poisoned the cross-iteration compare. Proper fix is in kloop/selfimprove.py (OUT of autonomous self-edit scope), proposed to human via journal, not applied to skills/CLAUDE.md.

analysis: iter3 +1.39 LB (7184.21->7185.60, recover bronze) from submitting held-back iter2 craft + fresh-gated frank/poby min-merge. Generalizable code-comp gotcha: some file-based comps enforce an EXACT submission filename (submission.zip) -> a renamed upload 400s FAILED_PRECONDITION (not gate/auth). Added the precondition + fix to kaggloop-submit code-comp section.
rationale: any file-submission comp with a fixed required filename hits this; the fix (copy to required name, read it from competition.json submission.format) generalizes across comps

analysis: iter4 flat 7185.60 (breakthrough h0017 refuted: bitboard BFS 3.3x costlier + fresh-fail vs near-optimal MaxPool champion). No pipeline edit (results-gated). Prior round's dual-submission mandate edits did NOT regress the score (flat, not down) -> not reverted.
